question_structure.context: As shown in the figure, the horizontal conveyor belt is moving clockwise at a speed of $v=6\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}$. The distance between the two drive wheels $M$ and $N$ is $L_{1}=10\mathrm{m}$. If a object with mass $m=3\mathrm{kg}$ is gently placed on the conveyor belt directly above wheel $M$ (taking $g=10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$).
question_structure.sub_question_1: Given that the coefficient of kinetic friction between the object and the conveyor belt is $\mu{=}0.3$, how much work is done by the frictional force of the conveyor belt on the object as it is transported from point $M$ to point $N$?
question_structure.sub_question_2: The conditions of problem (1) remain unchanged. If the conveyor belt speed is adjusted to $v^{\prime}{=}9\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}$, find the work done by the frictional force of the conveyor belt on the object.
question_structure.sub_question_3: If the coefficient of kinetic friction between the object and the conveyor belt is $\mu^{\prime}{=}0.1$, how much work is done by the frictional force of the conveyor belt on the object?

sub_question_1.step_1: When the object is first placed on the conveyor belt, there is relative sliding between the object and the conveyor belt. The acceleration of the object's motion is: $a=\frac{F}{m}=\frac{\mu m g}{m}=\mu g=3\mathrm{m/s^2}$
sub_question_1.step_2: When the object's velocity increases to match the speed of the conveyor belt, the frictional force disappears, and the duration of the sliding friction is: $t=\frac{v}{a}=\frac{6}{3}\mathrm{s}=2\mathrm{s}$
sub_question_1.step_3: During this time, the displacement of the object's motion is $L=\frac{1}{2}a t^2=6\mathrm{m}<10\mathrm{m}$.
sub_question_1.step_4: The work done by friction on the object is $W=F L=\mu m g L=54\mathrm{J}$.

sub_question_2.step_5: The conveyor belt speed is adjusted to $v^{\prime}=9\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}$, and the acceleration remains unchanged at $3\mathrm{m/s^2}$.
sub_question_2.step_6: The time required for the object's speed to increase to match the speed of the conveyor belt is: $t_1 = \frac{v^{\prime}}{a} = 3s$
sub_question_2.step_7: The displacement of the object's motion during this time: $L^{\prime}=\frac{1}{2}a t^{prime 2}=\frac{27}{2}\mathrm{m}>10\mathrm{m}$. From the actual situation, it is known that the true acting distance is $10\mathrm{m}$.
sub_question_2.step_8: The work done by the sliding friction force on the object is $W^{\prime}=F L_{1}=\mu m g L_{1}=90\mathrm{J}$.

sub_question_3.step_9: If $\mu^{\prime}=0.1$, then the acceleration of the object on the conveyor belt $a^{\prime}=\frac{F^{\prime}}{m}=\frac{\mu^{\prime}m g}{m}=\mu^{\prime}g=0.1\times 10\mathrm{m/s^{2}}=1\mathrm{m/s^{2}}$
sub_question_3.step_10: If the object's velocity can increase to $v=6\mathrm{m/s}$, then the displacement $L^{\prime\prime}=\frac{v^{2}}{2a^{\prime}}=\frac{6^{2}}{2{\times}1}\mathrm{m}=18\mathrm{m}>L_{1}$
sub_question_3.step_11: Therefore, the object accelerates from $M$ to $N$, and the work done by the sliding friction force on the object is $W^{\prime\prime}=F^{\prime}L_{1}=\mu^{\prime}m g L_{1}=0.1{\times}3{\times}10{\times}10\mathrm{J}=30\mathrm{J}$.

image_captions: The image depicts a conveyor belt system. The belt is horizontal and moves rightwards, indicated by an arrow and the letter 'v'. The belt is supported by two wheels, labeled 'M' and 'N'. A rectangular block sits on the left edge of the belt, above wheel 'M'.

result_quantity.name: Work done by friction on the object
result_quantity.equation: W = F L = \mu m g L

result_quantity.name: Time for the object's speed to match the conveyor belt
Work done by the sliding friction force on the object
result_quantity.equation: W^{\prime} = F L_{1} = \mu m g L_{1}

result_quantity.name: Acceleration of the object on the conveyor belt
Displacement of object when reaching v=6m/s
Work done by the sliding friction force on the object
